verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-related peptidase 2) is a secreted serine protease produced by prostate epithelium, synthesized as a pre-pro-enzyme that is processed and released into seminal fluid and circulation. It has no TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Walking the contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/cell-death/activation outer-leaflet display of the protein body; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — prostate-restricted expression, but as a secreted enzyme, not surface-anchored; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM minority pool; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent tethering to a TM partner documented. Note: clinical programs (e.g., JNJ-78278343 TCR/bispecific) target KLK2-derived pMHC peptides, which does not credit surface accessibility of the protein body.
